overall_justification: While the edition mentions the Caixa de Conversão and related concepts like "fixação do valor da moeda" and "conversão do papel moeda," these mentions are primarily factual and within broader discussions of government policy and economic matters. There is no clear editorial endorsement of either the Orthodox or Expansionist position. The article discussing Leopoldo de Bulhões' speech mentions the Caixa as one of several government initiatives, but doesn't argue for or against a specific conversion rate or underlying philosophy.  The other mentions are even more fleeting and neutral.

supporting_evidence.quote: Em breve, o serviço de descargas e embarque será iniciado em o novo caes, que as obras do porto já promptaram; o Banco do Brazil, reorganizado com a collaboração do Thesouro, prestará aos commerciantes os auxilios do credito que elles necessitam; e o governo vai estabelecer a caixa de compensação para movimento de cheques, com dispensa da intervenção do dinheiro do contado nas operações bancarias.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote from Leopoldo de Bulhões' speech mentions the Caixa de Conversão among other policy initiatives, but offers no specific argument for or against a particular approach to its implementation.

supporting_evidence.quote: A caixa de conversão preservará o ouro, mas que garantia temos nós brazileiros quanto a abolição do imposto de Buenos Aires...
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote raises a question about the impact of the Caixa de Conversão, but doesn't clearly advocate for either the Orthodox or Expansionist perspective.
